bio.bio_blurb: Founder of the Cowles Commission for economics

career.organization: Cowles Commission for Research in Economics

narrative_blocks.header: Could the stock forecasters actually forecast?
narrative_blocks.body: Convinced after the 1929 crash that he didn’t understand markets, he set out to test whether investment advisers beat random chance — and found they did not. To run the numbers he founded the Cowles Commission in 1932, bankrolling a brain trust that helped turn economics into a quantitative science.

narrative_blocks.header: He bankrolled the journal that built a field
narrative_blocks.body: He offered to underwrite a new journal, Econometrica, and the fledgling Econometric Society, in exchange for assembling leading scholars around his commission. The journal debuted in 1933 with Cowles himself as its circulation manager and treasurer.

narrative_blocks.header: He left the market just before it crashed
narrative_blocks.body: A Colorado Springs investment counselor, he pulled out of the financial markets before the Wall Street Crash of 1929. Growing skeptical of the advisers he’d once relied on, he closed his own forecasting newsletter in 1931, telling subscribers he simply did not know enough yet.

narrative_blocks.header: Tuberculosis sent him west to the mountains
narrative_blocks.body: Within two years of starting his own railroad-investment firm in Chicago he contracted tuberculosis and entered a Colorado Springs sanatorium for a year. The illness rerouted his whole life to Colorado, where the commission that bears his name was born.

narrative_blocks.header: A name three generations deep he set aside
narrative_blocks.body: He was the third Alfred Cowles in a row — grandfather, father, and son all tied to the Chicago Tribune — but he preferred to be known simply as Alfred, or “Bob” to his family. He and his wife Betsy adopted two children before their 1939 divorce.

burialCemetery.name: Lake Forest Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Lake Forest Cemetery, Grand Haven Ottawa County MI. Burial place of Olger sisters Harriet E. Olger Walker and Cuma M. Olger. Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.
